summary: Add 'todo pr' command: push branch + gh pr create with ticket-derived title/body
description:
New CLI command 'todo pr' that wraps the push + GitHub PR creation.

Behavior:
- Resolve current branch via git symbolic-ref --short HEAD. Must match
  todo/<id> pattern; error if not.
- Read the ticket (parent or single) by deriving id from branch name.
- Push branch to origin with -u (idempotent).
- Auto-generate PR title: ticket.summary (max ~70 chars).
- Auto-generate PR body:
  - For parent tickets: a 'Children' section listing each closed child
    with its resolution note + linked commit short SHA. Parent's own
    resolution note (if any) at the top.
  - For single tickets: description + resolution note + linked commit.
- Call 'gh pr create --title ... --body ...' via heredoc to preserve
  formatting. If a PR already exists for the branch, run 'gh pr edit'
  to update title/body instead (detect via 'gh pr view --json url').
- Print the PR URL on stdout.
- Flags: --base <branch> (default: detected default branch),
  --draft (open as draft PR).

Errors clearly when:
- gh CLI missing → message with install pointer
- No git remote → message
- Not on a todo/<id> branch → message

Done contract: tests/pr.test.ts covers (a) title/body generation from a
parent ticket with children, (b) branch detection (refuses on main),
(c) gh-missing error path (mock execFileSync), (d) PR-exists update path.
Mocking 'gh' is fine — we're testing the wrapper logic, not GitHub.

resolution.test_function: creates a PR when none exists, returns URL
resolution.note: todo pr ships: push -u origin + gh pr create with ticket-derived title/body. Parent tickets get a Children section. PR-exists path runs gh pr edit. 16 new tests; suite 187 passing.
